verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he brought him to Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When God in the beginning made man, He did not suffer him to be alone, but gave him woman for a helpmate, and made them to dwell together, knowing that great advantage would result from this companionship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What though the woman did not rightly employ this benefit? still if any one make himself fully acquainted with the nature of the matter, he will see, that to the wise great advantage arises from this dwelling together; not in the cause of wife or husband only, but if brothers do this, they also shall enjoy the benefit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Prophet hath said, “What is good, what is pleasant, but that brethren should dwell together?” ( Ps. cxxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Paul exhorted not to neglect the assembling of ourselves together. ( Heb. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this it is that we differ from beasts, for this we have built cities, and markets, and houses, that we may be united one with another, not in the place of our dwelling only, but by the bond of love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since our nature came imperfect from Him who made it, and is not self-sufficient, God, for our advantage, ordained that the want hence existing should be corrected by the assistance arising from mutual intercourse; so that what was lacking in one should be supplied by another, and the defective nature thus be rendered self-sufficient; as, for instance, that though made mortal, it should by succession for a long time maintain immortality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I might have gone into this argument at greater length, to show what advantages arise to those who come together from genuine and pure intercourse with each other: but there is another thing which presses now, that on account of which we have made these remarks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Andrew, after having tarried with Jesus and learned what He did, kept not the treasure to himself, but hastens and runs quickly to his brother, to impart to him of the good things which he had received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore has not John said on what matters Christ conversed with them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence is it clear that it was for this that they “abode with Him”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was proved by us the other day; but we may learn it from what has been read today as well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe what Andrew says to his brother; “We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: You see how, as far as he had learned in a short time, he showed the wisdom of the teacher who persuaded them, and their own zeal, who cared for these things long ago, and from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this word, “we have found,” is the expression of a soul which travails for His presence, and looks for His coming from above, and is made overjoyed when the looked-for thing has happened, and hastens to impart to others the good tidings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the part of brotherly affection, of natural friendship, of a sincere disposition, to be eager to stretch out the hand to each other in spiritual things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear him besides speak with the addition of the article; for he does not say “Messias,” but “the Messias”; thus they were expecting some one Christ, having nothing in common with the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And behold, I beg of you, the mind of Peter obedient and tractable from the very beginning; he ran to Him without any delay; “He brought him,” saith St. John, “to Jesus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet let no one blame his easy temper if he received the word without much questioning, because it is probable that his brother had told him these things more exactly and at length; but the Evangelists from their care for conciseness constantly cut many things short.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, it is not said absolutely that “he believed,” but that “he brought him to Jesus,” to give him up for the future to Him, so that from Him he might learn all; for the other disciple also was with him, and contributed to this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if John the Baptist, when he had said that He was “the Lamb,” and that He “baptized with the Spirit,” gave them over to learn the clearer doctrine concerning this thing from Him, much more would Andrew have done this, not deeming him self sufficient to declare the whole, but drawing him to the very fount of light with so much zeal and joy, that the other neither deferred nor delayed at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And when Jesus beheld him,” saith the Evangelist, “He said, Thou art Simon, the son of Jonas; thou shalt be called Cephas, which is, by interpretation, a stone.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He begins from this time forth to reveal the things belonging to His Divinity, and to open It out little by little by predictions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So He did in the case of Nathaniel and the Samaritan woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For prophecies bring men over not less than miracles; and are free from the appearance of boasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Miracles may possibly be slandered among foolish men, (“He casteth out devils,” said they, “by Beelzebub”— Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ), but nothing of the kind has ever been said of prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now in the case of Nathaniel and Simon He used this method of teaching, but with Andrew and Philip He did not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because those (two) had the testimony of John, no small preparation, and Philip received a credible evidence of faith, when he saw those who had been present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou art Simon, the son of Jonas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By the present, the future is guaranteed; for it is clear that He who named Peter’s father foreknew the future also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the prediction is attended with praise; but the object was not to flatter, but to foretell something future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear at least in the case of the Samaritan woman, how He utters a prediction with severe reproofs; “Thou hast had,” he saith, “five husbands, and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband.” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So also His Father makes great account of prophecy, when He sets Himself against the honor paid to idols: “Let them declare to you,” saith He, “what shall come upon you” ( Isa. xlvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ); and again, “I have declared, and have saved, and there was no foreign God amongst you” ( Isa. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 12 , LXX.); and He brings this forward through all prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because prophecy is especially the work of God, which devils cannot even imitate, though they strive exceedingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in the case of miracles there may be delusion; but exactly to foretell the future belongs to that pure Nature alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if devils ever have done so, it was by deceiving the simpler sort; whence their oracles are always easily detected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Peter makes no reply to these words; as yet he knew nothing clearly, but still was learning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, that not even the prediction is fully set forth; for Jesus did not say, “I will change thy name to Peter, and upon this rock I will build My Church,” but, “Thou shalt be called Cephas.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The former speech would have expressed too great authority and power; for Christ does not immediately nor at first declare all His power, but speaks for a while in a humbler tone; and so, when He had given the proof of His Divinity, He puts it more authoritatively, saying, “Blessed art thou, Simon, because My Father hath revealed it to thee”; and again, “Thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My Church.” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Him therefore He so named, and James and his brother He called “sons of thunder.” ( Mark iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then doth He this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that it was He who gave the old covenant, that it was He who altered names, who called Abram “Abraham,” and Sarai “Sarah,” and Jacob “Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To many he assigned names even from their birth, as to Isaac, and Samson, and to those in Isaiah and Hosea ( Isa. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4, 6, 9 ); but to others He gave them after they had been named by their parents, as to those we have mentioned, and to Joshua the son of Nun.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was also a custom of the Ancients to give names from things, which in fact Leah also has done; and this takes place not without cause, but in order that men may have the appellation to remind them of the goodness of God, that a perpetual memory of the prophecy conveyed by the names may sound in the ears of those who receive it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus too He named John early, because they whose virtue was to shine forth from their early youth, from that time received their names; while to those who were to become great at a later period, the title also was given later.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But then they received each a different name, we now have all one name, that which is greater than any, being called “Christians,” and “sons of God,” and (His) “friends,” and (His) “Body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the very term itself is able more than all those others to rouse us, and make us more zealous for the practice of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then act unworthily of the honor belonging to the title, considering the excess of our dignity, we who are called Christ’s; for so Paul hath named us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us bear in mind and respect the grandeur of the appellation. ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if one who is said to be descended from some famous general, or one otherwise distinguished, is proud to be called this or that man’s son, and deems the name a great honor, and strives in every way so as not to affix, by remissness of his own, reproach to him after whom he is called; shall not we who are called after the name, not of a general, nor any of the princes upon earth, nor Angel, nor Archangel, nor Seraphim, but of the King of these Himself, shall not we freely give even our very life, so as not to insult Him who has honored us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Know ye not what honor the royal bands of shield-bearers and spearmen that are about the king enjoy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So let us who have been deemed worthy to be near Him, and much closer, and as much nearer than those just named, as the body is closer to the head than they, let us, I say, use every means to be imitators of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.” ( Luke ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if I demand this of you, it will seem perhaps to most of you grievous and burdensome; because therefore of your infirmity I speak not of such perfection, but desire you not to be nailed to riches; and as I, because of the infirmity of the many, retire somewhat from (demanding) the excess of virtue, I desire that you do so and much more on the side of vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I blame not those who have houses, and lands, and wealth, and servants, but wish them to possess these things in a safe and becoming way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is “a becoming way”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As masters, not as slaves; so that they rule them, be not ruled by them; that they use, not abuse them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is why they are called, “things to be used,” that we may employ them on necessary services, not hoard them up; this is a domestic’s office, that a master’s; it is for the slave to keep them, but for the lord and one who has great authority to expend.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst not receive thy wealth to bury, but to distribute.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had God desired riches to be hoarded, He would not have given them to men, but would have let them remain as they were in the earth; but because He wishes them to be spent, therefore He has permitted us to have them, that we may impart them to each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we keep them to ourselves, we are no longer masters of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if you wish to make them greater and therefore keep them shut up, even in this case the best plan of all is to scatter and distribute them in all directions; because there can be no revenue without an outlay, no wealth without expenditure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: One may see that it is so even in worldly matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So it is with the merchant, so with the husbandman, who put forth the one his wealth, the other his seed; the one sails the sea to disperse his wares, the other labors all the year putting in and tending his seed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here there is no need of any one of these things, neither to equip a vessel, nor to yoke oxen, nor to plough land, nor to be anxious about uncertain weather, nor to dread a fall of hail; here are neither waves nor rocks; this voyage and this sowing needs one thing only, that we cast forth our possessions; all the rest will that Husbandman do, of whom Christ saith, “My Father is the Husbandman.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not then absurd to be sluggish and slothful where we may gain all without labor, and where there are many toils and many troubles and cares, and after all, an uncertain hope, there to display all eagerness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not, I beseech you, let us not be to such a degree senseless about our own salvation, but let us leave the more troublesome task, and run to that which is most easy and more profitable, that we may obtain also the good things that are to come; through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy and quickening Spirit be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
